tweets.title: EAM Dr. S. Jaishankar met with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi to review the gradual normalization of India-China relations since the October 2024 Kazan summit. India’s stance remains firm: peace and tranquility in the border areas is the absolute prerequisite for normal ties. EAM highlighted several concrete steps taken toward normalization over the past few months, including: ▪️ Resumption of direct flights between India and China ▪️ Restarting the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra ▪️ Recommencing bilateral border trade ▪️ Updating the visa regime Despite the recent border de-escalation, Jaishankar clearly outlined the remaining structural issues that India expects to be addressed: ▪️ Ensuring fair market access for Indian goods ▪️ Correcting the ongoing trade imbalance ▪️ Securing predictability in supply chains
tweets.content:
EAM Dr. S. Jaishankar met with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi to review the gradual normalization of India-China relations since the October 2024 Kazan summit.

India’s stance remains firm: peace and tranquility in the border areas is the absolute prerequisite for normal ties.

EAM highlighted several concrete steps taken toward normalization over the past few months, including:
▪️ Resumption of direct flights between India and China
▪️ Restarting the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra
▪️ Recommencing bilateral border trade
▪️ Updating the visa regime

Despite the recent border de-escalation, Jaishankar clearly outlined the remaining structural issues that India expects to be addressed:
▪️ Ensuring fair market access for Indian goods
▪️ Correcting the ongoing trade imbalance
▪️ Securing predictability in supply chains

tweets.title: BREAKING: Terrorists Attack J&K Police Party in Anantnag Terrorists opened fire on a Jammu & Kashmir Police party at Lal Chowk, Anantnag, along the Kokernag–Anantnag Road, around 12:30 PM on July 22. Head Constable Ashiq Hussain of the IRP 3rd Battalion, who was deployed for Amarnath Yatra security, sustained critical injuries and later succumbed at GMC Anantnag. Police personnel returned fire, but the terrorists managed to flee the scene. Additional security forces have been rushed to the area, and a massive search operation is underway to trace the attackers.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Terrorists Attack J&K Police Party in Anantnag

Terrorists opened fire on a Jammu & Kashmir Police party at Lal Chowk, Anantnag, along the Kokernag–Anantnag Road, around 12:30 PM on July 22.

Head Constable Ashiq Hussain of the IRP 3rd Battalion, who was deployed for Amarnath Yatra security, sustained critical injuries and later succumbed at GMC Anantnag.

Police personnel returned fire, but the terrorists managed to flee the scene. Additional security forces have been rushed to the area, and a massive search operation is underway to trace the attackers.

Key provisions: • No written examination • Exemption from Physical Standards Test (PST) and Physical Efficiency Test (PET) • 3-year age relaxation for all Ex-Agniveers • 5-year one-time age relaxation for the first Agniveer batch • A dedicated Ex-Agniveer Wing has been created in the Ministry of Home Affairs. The first batch of Agniveers has not yet completed its four-year tenure, so the policy is yet to be implemented through its first recruitment cycle.
tweets.content:
Government Introduces 50% CAPF Quota for Ex-Agniveers

The Government has informed Parliament that 50% of Constable (GD) and Rifleman vacancies in the CRPF, BSF, CISF, ITBP, SSB, and Assam Rifles will be reserved for Ex-Agniveers.

Key provisions:
• No written examination
• Exemption from Physical Standards Test (PST) and Physical Efficiency Test (PET)
• 3-year age relaxation for all Ex-Agniveers
• 5-year one-time age relaxation for the first Agniveer batch
• A dedicated Ex-Agniveer Wing has been created in the Ministry of Home Affairs.

The first batch of Agniveers has not yet completed its four-year tenure, so the policy is yet to be implemented through its first recruitment cycle.

tweets.title: We often talk about defence and space together, so here’s an interesting comparison. A few days ago, Vikram-1 made its first flight. If we draw a chronological analogy with defence, it is comparable to an LCA MK1 class milestone. However, in defence, we have already invited a private-sector consortium to lead production of AMCA, a programme that, in terms of ambition and complexity, is far closer to SpaceX’s Starship (even though the design authority remains with DRDO). That’s the difference. In space, private industry is first proving itself on smaller launch vehicles before moving to heavy-lift systems. In defence, we’re asking private industry to participate in manufacturing one of our most ambitious combat aircraft programmes from the outset. This is exactly what we had been saying for years: we haven’t fed our industry the basic tech and forcing them into AMCA. This is why space has revived early success in private efforts.
tweets.content:
We often talk about defence and space together, so here’s an interesting comparison.

A few days ago, Vikram-1 made its first flight. If we draw a chronological analogy with defence, it is comparable to an LCA MK1 class milestone.

However, in defence, we have already invited a private-sector consortium to lead production of AMCA, a programme that, in terms of ambition and complexity, is far closer to SpaceX’s Starship (even though the design authority remains with DRDO).

That’s the difference.

In space, private industry is first proving itself on smaller launch vehicles before moving to heavy-lift systems. In defence, we’re asking private industry to participate in manufacturing one of our most ambitious combat aircraft programmes from the outset.

This is exactly what we had been saying for years: we haven’t fed our industry the basic tech and forcing them into AMCA. This is why space has revived early success in private efforts.

tweets.title: India Rules Out Return to Existing Indus Waters Treaty India has ruled out restoring the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in its current form, with any future agreement contingent on Pakistan credibly and permanently ending cross-border terrorism. New Delhi is also accelerating five hydropower projects on the Chenab River with a combined capacity of 4,870 MW, aimed at strengthening water security, clean energy generation, and fuller utilisation of India's rights in the Indus basin. The projects include Sawalkote (1,856 MW), Pakal Dul (1,000 MW), Ratle (850 MW), Kiru (624 MW), and Kwar (540 MW). Four are under construction, while Sawalkote remains in the clearance stage.
tweets.content:
India Rules Out Return to Existing Indus Waters Treaty

India has ruled out restoring the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in its current form, with any future agreement contingent on Pakistan credibly and permanently ending cross-border terrorism.

New Delhi is also accelerating five hydropower projects on the Chenab River with a combined capacity of 4,870 MW, aimed at strengthening water security, clean energy generation, and fuller utilisation of India's rights in the Indus basin.

That said, taunts like “hangar beauty” should be taken on the chin. Manufacturing and capability development is difficult to process for those who aren’t associated with the domain. Let the import bahadurs win today’s narrative so India can win more important war of Atmanirbharta. A year from now, the picture will likely look very different.
